notes: Jeopardy! NES is a quiz/trivia game show adaptation with no combat, danger states, factions, or political systems. The game lacks traditional manifestation layer elements (danger signals, faction identities, world state visualizations) because it operates as a static quiz interface rather than a dynamic world simulation. Visual communication is limited to: (1) question category board display, (2) score/contestant state indicators, (3) UI state changes (active/inactive buttons), and (4) branding elements (Jeopardy! logo with TM symbol revisions between ROM versions). The source material focuses on technical artifacts (unused Coca-Cola logo, revisional differences to trademark symbols) rather than gameplay-relevant visual communication systems. The game has no enemy factions, safety/danger states, or world-state dependent visual manifestations to extract.

data_limitations.explanation: Jeopardy! NES is a quiz show adaptation designed for educational gameplay without world-state systems, enemy factions, or danger/safety mechanics. The manifestation layer framework is designed for games with dynamic world systems, faction conflict, and environmental storytelling. This game communicates through static UI/game show mechanics, not world manifestation.
data_limitations.available_data: Logo branding elements and UI state indicators only
